title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flags two items: (1) **Warning** — the PR adds graceful-shutdown features but lacks a Chainsaw E2E test (`tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/`) that asserts the new Deployment spec fields (terminationGracePeriodSeconds, startupProbe, preStop hook) on a real cluster, unlike every other feature which has one. (2) **Info** — the startup probe's `TimeoutSeconds` defaults to 1s, which may be worth reconsidering.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 3a5c8bd-dirty with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=1 info=1 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (1)

### W-001: Missing Chainsaw E2E test for graceful-shutdown feature
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Enforce consistent error-handling pattern across all test cases

**Problem**: The project has 19 Chainsaw E2E test directories under tests/e2e/keystone/ covering every existing feature (autoscaling, basic-deployment, credential-rotation, fernet-rotation, network-policy, trust-flush, uwsgi, etc.). This PR adds a new operational feature but includes no corresponding E2E test that asserts the Deployment spec fields (terminationGracePeriodSeconds, startupProbe, lifecycle.preStop) on a real cluster. The existing basic-deployment chainsaw test only asserts availableReplicas > 0 and condition statuses — it does not inspect the pod spec.

**Code**:
```
// integration_test.go:1702-1704
// TestIntegration_GracefulShutdownSpec verifies that the preStop lifecycle hook,
// terminationGracePeriodSeconds, and startup probe survive a full reconciliation
// cycle through the API server (CC-0063, REQ-006).
```

**Action Required**: Add a tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/ directory with a chainsaw-test.yaml that applies a Keystone CR and asserts the Deployment spec contains the expected terminationGracePeriodSeconds, startupProbe, and lifecycle.preStop fields — matching the pattern used by other E2E tests in the same suite.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add a tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/ directory with a chainsaw-test.yaml that applies a Keystone CR and asserts the Deployment spec contains the expected terminationGracePeriodSeconds, startupProbe, and lifecycle.preStop fields.
```

---

## INFO (1)

### I-001: Startup probe TimeoutSeconds defaults to 1s
**File**: `reconcile_deployment.go:206-215` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: uncertain

**Problem**: The startup probe does not set TimeoutSeconds explicitly. The Kubernetes default is 1 second. During cold starts — specifically the scenario this probe is designed for (large DB, cold Memcached caches) — the /v3 endpoint might take longer than 1 second to respond if it performs any backend initialization on the first request.

**Code**:
```
StartupProbe: &corev1.Probe{
    ProbeHandler: corev1.ProbeHandler{
        HTTPGet: &corev1.HTTPGetAction{
            Path: "/v3",
            Port: intstr.FromInt32(5000),
        },
    },
    FailureThreshold: 30,
    PeriodSeconds:    10,
},
```

**Action Required**: No action required now. Monitor for slow-start timeouts in cold-start scenarios. If observed, consider setting TimeoutSeconds to a higher value (e.g. 3-5s). The risk is mitigated by the generous FailureThreshold of 30 and the fact that the probe only needs an HTTP response code.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
If slow-start timeouts are observed, add TimeoutSeconds: 5 to the startup probe configuration.
```

---

## Summary

This is a clean, well-scoped PR that correctly implements graceful shutdown with a 5-second preStop sleep for kube-proxy convergence, a 30-second terminationGracePeriodSeconds for uWSGI drain time, and a startup probe with a 5-minute window to prevent liveness-kill loops during cold starts. The code follows project conventions (ptr.To usage) and includes thorough unit and integration tests. The only meaningful gap is the absence of a Chainsaw E2E test, which the project consistently provides for every other feature.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 1 |
| INFO     | 1 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Merge after adding a Chainsaw E2E test for the graceful-shutdown feature to maintain the project's established testing standard. The INFO finding about startup probe TimeoutSeconds is optional and can be addressed later if cold-start timeouts are observed in practice.
resolution_summary: Added a Chainsaw E2E test directory tests/e2e/keystone/graceful-shutdown/ with chainsaw-test.yaml and 00-keystone-cr.yaml to address W-001. The test applies a minimal Keystone CR, waits for Ready status, then asserts the Deployment spec contains terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30, preStop exec sleep 5, and startupProbe httpGet /v3:5000 with failureThreshold 30 and periodSeconds 10 — matching the pattern used by other E2E tests in the suite. I-001 (startup probe TimeoutSeconds defaults to 1s) was not addressed, which is appropriate for an INFO-level observation with uncertain confidence.
